mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of decision date indicates ultra-low rate environment
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS < 0.5% periods do not exceed baseline by at least 50 basis points (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market strength that sustains consumer spending and corporate profit growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 133,009 thousand as of decision date
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when PAYEMS monthly growth exceeds 150,000, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth acceleration above 0.5% month-over-month indicates strengthening consumer demand and forward earnings visibility, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 404,596 million dollars as of decision date
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or correlation between RSAFS growth and subsequent SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index decline below 97.0 signals weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side constraints that compress profit margins and reduce equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.2853 as of decision date, near trigger threshold
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of instances when INDPRO < 97.0, or Rank IC > -0.02 (insufficient negative predictive power) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rise above 4.5% combined with month-over-month payroll decline signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.2% as of decision date indicates elevated unemployment
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of jointly triggered instances (both conditions met), or the interaction effect (joint signal) does not produce lower returns than either signal alone (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline below 1,000 thousand units combined with retail sales weakness signals demand-side contraction that reduces construction employment and consumer wealth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 717 thousand units as of decision date
regime_hint: demand-side recession or housing market contraction
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 52% of jointly triggered instances, or the combined signal produces no statistically significant return differential versus baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI acceleration above 2.5% year-over-year combined with elevated federal funds rate above 2.0% signals monetary tightening cycle that increases discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 228.527 and FEDFUNDS at 0.16% as of decision date
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation concerns
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of jointly triggered instances, or the interaction term does not explain additional variance beyond individual series (R² improvement < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth deceleration below 2.0% annualized signals weakening economic momentum that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days in late-cycle expansion regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,491 billion chained 2017 dollars as of decision date
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 (insufficient predictive power) over any 3-year rolling window.
